campus: University of Bath, Claverton Down, Bath BA2 7AY

requirements.undergrad_background: 2:2 to 2:1 or above (or international equivalent) undergraduate degree depending on institution attended; degree can be in any discipline but a quantitative background/aptitude is required (e.g. engineering, mathematics, economics, physics; other subjects considered if quantitative content sufficient).
requirements.gpa_requirement: Chinese qualifications: a four-year Bachelor's degree with a final overall score of at least 62-75%, depending on the institution attended. No named tier list of Chinese universities was found on the official page — only this percentage range differentiated by 'institution attended'.

ielts.subscores: Reading, Writing, Listening, Speaking each at least 6.0; results no more than 2 years old at course start. PTE Academic, TOEFL iBT, CPE, CAE also accepted. Combined pre-sessional offers: IELTS 6.0 overall (5.5 each skill) = 5-week pre-sessional; IELTS 5.5 overall (5.5 each skill) = 10-week pre-sessional.

toefl.subscores: TOEFL iBT accepted as an alternative to IELTS but exact score threshold not stated on this page — 官网未列明具体TOEFL分数

requirements.gmat_gre: Not mentioned anywhere on the official course, entry-requirements, or application pages — 官网未列明，推断不作为该项目的必需材料
requirements.prerequisites: Quantitative background required; professional accreditation with ACCA currently in progress (outcome pending).
requirements.work_experience: Not required by default; 'in exceptional cases, relevant work experience may be a substitute for the degree requirement' (max 3 years' relevant experience).

deadlines.notes: Course page 'Application information' section lists Home application deadline 31 August 2026 and Overseas application deadline 30 June 2026, with note 'we recommend you apply early as we may close applications before the deadline if a course is full.' Separately, School of Management scholarship deadlines are 4 February 2026 (round 1) and 8 April 2026 (round 2, final). Note: a separate university-wide 'Taught postgraduate admissions timeline' page states a later generic overseas deadline of 31 July 2026 — the course-specific page (30 June 2026) is treated as authoritative here since it is course-level data.

notes: All figures read directly from the live 2026/27 course page (interactively selecting 'China' in the 'Origin of qualifications' dropdown, which is JS-driven and not visible via static fetch) and the official School of Management 2026/27 fees page. Materials list sourced from the general 'Applying for a taught postgraduate course' guide (university-wide, applies to School of Management applicants).
